segments.text: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
to know wisdom and instruction; to discern the words of understanding;
to receive instruction in wise dealing, in righteousness, justice, and equity;
to give prudence to the simple, knowledge and discretion to the young man—
that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning; that the man of understanding may attain to sound counsel;
to understand a proverb and parables, the words and riddles of the wise.
The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of knowledge, but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.

footnotes.text: + 'Yahweh  is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered  'LORD (all caps) in other translations.

segments.text: My son, listen to your father’s instruction, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching;
for they will be a garland to grace your head, and chains around your neck.
My son, if sinners entice you, don’t consent.
If they say,

Come with us. Let’s lie in wait for blood. 
Let’s lurk secretly for the innocent without cause.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,  and whole, like those who go down into the pit.
segments.speaker: Sinners

footnotes.text: + Sheol is the place of the dead.

segments.text: We’ll find all valuable wealth. We’ll fill our houses with plunder.
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: You shall cast your lot among us. We’ll all have one purse—
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: my son, don’t walk on the path with them. Keep your foot from their path,
for their feet run to evil. They hurry to shed blood.
For the net is spread in vain in the sight of any bird;
but these lay in wait for their own blood. They lurk secretly for their own lives.
So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain. It takes away the life of its owners.
Wisdom calls aloud in the street. She utters her voice in the public squares.
She calls at the head of noisy places. At the entrance of the city gates, she utters her words:

How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? How long will mockers delight themselves in mockery, 
and fools hate knowledge?
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text:
Turn at my reproof. Behold,  I will pour out my spirit on you. 
I will make known my words to you.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

footnotes.text: + 'Behold , from  'הִנֵּה, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

segments.text: Because I have called, and you have refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no one has paid attention;
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: but you have ignored all my counsel, and wanted none of my reproof;
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: I also will laugh at your disaster. I will mock when calamity overtakes you,
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text:
when calamity overtakes you like a storm, when your disaster comes on like a whirlwind, 
when distress and anguish come on you.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Then they will call on me, but I will not answer. They will seek me diligently, but they will not find me,
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: because they hated knowledge, and didn’t choose the fear of Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: They wanted none of my counsel. They despised all my reproof.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own schemes.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them. The careless ease of fools will destroy them.
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely, and will be at ease, without fear of harm.
segments.speaker: Wisdom
